verdict_reasoning: TMED9 (transmembrane emp24 domain-containing protein 9, also p24 family member) is a type-I single-pass transmembrane protein that resides in the early secretory pathway — predominantly ER and Golgi membranes — where it functions as a cargo receptor/adaptor in COPI- and COPII-mediated vesicle trafficking. Its lumenal p24 domain faces the Golgi/ER lumen (not the extracellular space), and its cytoplasmic tail mediates coat-protein interactions. It does not traffic to the plasma membrane under baseline conditions. Contextual buckets: no documented cancer/stress ecto-pool; no germline/developmental surface display; not a lysosomal TM protein recycled to PM; no documented PM minority pool or cycling; no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering at the cell surface. The transmembrane domain is present but the protein is endomembrane-resident, not surface-accessible.
